task.prompt: Write a function `parse_tags(text)` that parses a comma-separated tag string.

gold_spec.goal: Parse comma-separated tags while honoring backslash-escaped commas and backslashes.
postconditions: Scan the input left to right.
A comma is a separator only when it is preceded by an even number of consecutive backslashes, including zero.
A comma preceded by an odd number of consecutive backslashes is data and the escaping backslash is removed.
After splitting, strip leading and trailing ASCII whitespace from each raw field.
Ignore fields that become empty after stripping.
Within each kept field, unescape '\\' to a single backslash and '\,' to a comma.
A backslash before any other character is preserved as a literal backslash.
Preserve case, duplicates, and item order.
edge_cases: Escaped commas do not split a tag.
Two backslashes before a comma mean the comma still splits.
A trailing escaped comma is part of the final tag, not a separator.
acceptance_criteria: The parser uses escape-aware scanning rather than a plain string split.

known_weakness.description: The raw prompt makes a plain split look plausible but the hidden semantics require odd/even backslash accounting, trimming, and selective unescaping.
known_weakness.naive_spec_gap: missing escaped delimiter and backslash normalization semantics
